publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-04-08 23:59:14 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Includes 24 scrapbooks and 12 notebooks compiled by Mulks with material relating to Tompkins County and nearby areas..

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Charles Freer Mulks was born in Slaterville Springs, Tompkins County, New York, and lived much of his life in nearby Ithaca. A descendant of Benoni Mulks, an early settler of the area, Mulks was interested in the history of Tompkins County, and kept a record of family histories and local events in a series of notebooks and scrapbooks.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Includes 24 scrapbooks and 12 notebooks compiled by Mulks, including notes on the settlement, migration, and treaties of Indians; Caroline, New York, slave population; local folklore; histories of many prominent families of Tompkins County, including the Ellis, Meddaugh (Middaugh), Mandeville, Mulks, Cornell, DeWitt, Speed, Thomas, and Cantine families; personal material; and notes on many other topics. Also, biographical sketch of Charles F. Mulks by Jane P. M. Vaughn (1975); photograph of Mulks; documents concerning the acqusition of land in the towns of Caroline (1800-38) and Ulysses (1833), New York; promissory notes, Dutchess County (1799, 1809); Erie Canal Directory and Rate Schedule (1847); internal revenue licenses (1860s); Phillips family estate settlement papers; and Thomas family genealogical data.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Charles F. Mulks papers, #3654. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also Martin P. Catherwood faculty biographical folder, #47-10-3394, for information compiled from Mulks' papers on the Morrell Mansion or Cornell Mansion, moved to 304 Highgate Road, Ithaca.

controlaccess.subject: Slave records -- New York -- Caroline.
Real property, Exchange of -- New York -- Caroline.
Indians of North America -- New York -- Treaties
Indians of North America -- New York (State)
Folklore -- New York -- Tompkins County.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbooks containing newspaper clippings, manuscript material, and printed material such as trade cards. Some of them are repurposed account books, with notes on the original pages.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 6 (formerly Harris 5135 G106); Clippings pasted throughout an old account book dated 1878 ; from the Ithaca Journal and other Ithaca and Syracuse newspapers; p. 108: two checks dated 1831 and 1833, drawn on Bank of Ithaca and signed by James Mulks

did.unittitle: Newspaper clippings: first third on the Rulloff case

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 7 (formerly Harris 5135 G107); Primarily from Broome County; remainder of clippings general/on history of New York State towns and villages

did.unittitle: Newspaper clippings: Rulloff case; politics and history of various New York State and other localities

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 8 (formerly Harris 5135 G108); Clipping-free pages at front of volume contain documents relating to the Town of Caroline: its history; minutes of town meetings; list of Supervisors and Town Clerks (1811-1869); plus a list of the officers of Tompkins County (1817-1870)

did.unittitle: Newspaper clippings, primarily on Tompkins County history

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 9 (formerly Harris 5135 G109; Written in script on the flyleaf: " Samuel P. Ashley's Ledger, July 12, 1837[1887?], Speedsville in Caroline, Tompkins County and State of New York, $3.50"); includes series of articles entitled "Centennial History of the Town of Dryden, " including stories of First Citizens and other Historical notes (ca. 1892, 1896); references to the Bates case in the Town of Caroline [see Notebook 5]

did.unittitle: Newspaper clippings, primarily from the "Journal of Commerce" (question and answer columns); Political cartoon from the Elmira Telegram

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 10 (formerly Harris 5135 G110); Includes an account of Jay Gould's last will and testament; Horace White's address on banking (Jan. 12, 1893); on front page is a circular letter from the N.Y. Tribune business superintendent, Henry Hall[?] to S. P. Ashley, Tribune agent at Slaterville, re inducements offered to acquire additional subscribers (March 9, 1887)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 11 (formerly Harris 5135 G111); Clippings from "Journal of Commerce" (question and answer columns) and re local and national history; mss include: bills of sale for goods and produce; checks drawn on Bank of Ithaca, and various other business documents either signed by or in the name of James Mulks (1827-1832); Caroline school reports (1814)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 12 (formerly Harris 5135 G112); Clippings from "Journal of Commerce" (question and answer columns, 1882-1885); mss and documents: accounts of Brinley Library sale; stock certificates issued to Edward Patrick for shares in the Gazette Co.; transfer document, of certificate, to Hon. Wm. Tweed (1870)

did.unittitle: Newspaper clippings, manuscripts, and printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 13 (formerly Harris 5135 G113); Clippings from "Journal of Commerce" (1899); mss copy of a history of Dryden that originallyappeared in the N.Y.S. Gazetteer; printed list of Officers of the Village of Ithaca for the years 1821-1868; several pages from the N.Y.S. Assembly Journal for the years 1808-1809; undated D. & H. [Delaware & Hudson] R.R. timetable, the back of which contains a map of the entire line

did.unittitle: Newspaper clippings, almost entirely political cartoons from the Owego Gazette, the Elmira Telegram, and other regional newspapers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 14 (formerly Harris 5135 G114); Includes Ithaca politics; cartoon by Judge Jason P. Merrill in the Elmira Telegram; references by Merrill to the Ben Mintz store, to the "Cornell Library Rostrum" (1892); photo of Judge Merrill; picture of the Senate with Vice President Stevenson in the Owego Gazette (Dec. 3, 1895)

did.unittitle: Newspaper clippings; manuscripts; legal documents; trade cards; political and social notices, announcements, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 15 (formerly Harris 5135 G115); Map of N.Y. State; Caroline political party tickets (1881, 1882); Caroline town officers majorities, Feb. (1884); Owego-Bath Mail Stage (March 1819); Caroline Temperance Society (1831); Rep. Jeremiah W. Dwight, speech on canals in Congress (1892); MIchigan Gov. Austin Blair (Caroline native) spoke at Eaton County, Michigan Pioneers picnic (June 1882); Speedsville Post Office Financial Report (April 1 -June 30, 1853); Application for widow's pension by Elizabeth Personius, widow of James Personius, a member of Capt. Cantine and DeForest's Co., N.Y. Militia, War of 1812; Transfer of property in Tioga County, now Caroline in Tompkins County, from John James and Mary T. Speed to Jonathan Norwood (May 29, 1819); Articles of Agreement between Robert Livingston and Josiah Perry, about 49 acres in town of Caroline (March 1830); License to practice law issued to S. P. Ashley, Speedsville (June 5, 1865[?]); Marriage of Steven Faville to Mrs. Ruth Latimer, sister of Charles Freer Mulks (April 29, 1886); Marriage invitation, Kate L. Ashley to W. R. Gunderman (Feb. 2, 1881); Invitation card to the Opening Part, Magnetic Springs House, Slaterville (Tues. eve. Feb. 19, 1878); Local talent show, Slaterville, names of players (Apr. 10, 1885); "Foully Murdered": special Spencer dispatch to the Journal about shooting of Joel Evans by J. F. Courtright, Speedsville, Oct. 7, 1882 (Oct. 10, 1882); Failure of D. B. Gilbert, Speedsville-story complementary (Apr. 29, 1886); The Perry Murder Trial; Bank checks, notes, legal notices signed by James Mulks, others; Slaterville personal: Scott Smalley of Kendaia, N.Y. and William Smalley of Eaton Rapids, Mich. visit sister Mrs. W. W. White and Mrs. Charles Northrup; "A Million Dollar College" (about Cornell), N. Y. Sun (1881); 26 chapters of Captain Mayne Reid's "The Rural Life of England," from the N.Y. Weekly Tribune (1882); J. D. Mandeville's "Out West!"; A Yankee Doodle Story

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 16 (formerly Harris 5135 G116); Dist. No. 2 Caroline School Report by Jro.[Jethro?] Mulks (March 3, 1821); List of all candidates for Caroline Town offices, three parties (1881); List of elderly people living in Danby, Groton, and Dryden (1881); Silas Richards, of Ridgefield, Fairfield County, Conn., appointed administrator of Nathaniel Richard's estate (Sept. 30, 1808); Silas Richards's accounts in Caroline; Notices from the Owego Gazette (Tues., July 6, 1819); Notice of Fourth of July celebration at Caroline, in the Tompkins American (June 25, 1834); Card for a Social Party, Freer's Hall, Slaterville (Jan. 1, 1880); Photo of James A. Garfield, Republican candidate for President (1880); Dewitt Park: news story on ownership of; Webster's "Seventeen Murders"; Facsimile of rough draft of account of Surrender at Yorktown

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 17 (formerly Harris 5135 G117); Caroline Supervisorship, in the Ithaca Democrat (Nov. 8, 1892); the Bates case; "Ithaca Eligibles" - list of bachelors, Ithaca Journal (Feb. 4, 1888)

did.unittitle: Newspaper clippings; bills and receipts; political cartoons; market price quotations; broadsides

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 18 (formerly Harris 5135 G118); Contemporary life of Indians; Ithaca Land Company map showing both existing and proposed street car lines; Ithaca Land Company map showing development on South Hill; Marriage of Henry G. Norwood to Hattie Lounsbery, a Cantine descendant, Ithaca Journal (Sept. 26, 1884); Picture of Cornell University

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 19 (formerly Harris 5135 G119); Early Reminiscences and Recollections by W. T. Eddy and others (Ithaca Journal columns); "Free whites" data from 1790 Census of Ulster County, NY (New Burgh, Marbletown; Charles Deniston); [Description/ account of] Lansing, NY (Spring 1791); Petition of the towns of Caroline, Danby, and Newfield to become part of Tompkins County (in the N.Y. Senate Journal, 1822); Plan for emigrating to the Territory of Michigan, by John Mulks, Wm. R. Collins, and Ira Tillotson, agents (Ithaca Journal, Dec. 8, 1824); Catskill-Ithaca Turnpike, by editor of the Stamford [Delaware County, NY] Mirror (n.d.); Ithaca Railroad History, early; L. C. Smith Typewriter Factory (story and pictures); Caroline Telegraph Company letterhead (early 1900s); Twelve Valiant Brothers of Trumansburg Masonic Lodge, no. 157, with pictures (following the Morgan Episode); Autographs of inspectors of elections on election returns in Ulysses and Lansing (Apr. 26, 1821) and in Caroline and elsewhere (Nov. 5, 1827); Peleg Ellis's Commission as Captain of the Cayuga County Militia, with seal and signed by Gov. Daniel D. Tompkins; Lt. Col. Henry Bloom, Commandant (Apr. 26, 1813); Postmaster Roswell Beardsley, Lansing (story and pictures); State Street, Ithaca, N.Y. (picture, 1860); Dewitt Family genealogy; Death notices and obituaries: Mary Ackley; Chauncey Mulks, wealthy Syracuse recluse, with picture (1903); E. Asbury Robison, grandson of early Caroline settler, John Robison (Aug. 20, 1903[?]); James F. Latimer, of Delaware, Wisc. (Nov. 11, 1904); Edward Lounsbury, son of early Caroline settler, Peter Lounsbury (ca. 1904); Gov. Alonzo B. Cornell (Oct. 5, 1907); Estate of Peleg Ellis's daughter, Ann H. Smith (d. 1900); Removal of Simeon DeWitt's body (in the Ithaca Democrat, June 4, 1924); County Treasurer Ingersoll Scandal (1900); Benoni Mulks; Rev. Samuel J. Parker (story, Apr. 4, 1899); Tompkins County Agricultural Society meeting (1904); Dewitt Historical Society Officers appointed by Pres. Griffis (Apr. 5, 1900[?]); Leavenworth Mansion, Finest Frame House in the World (Nov. 10, 1904); "Voitlander and I in Pursuit of Shadow Castings," by James F. Ryder, roller boy at the Ithaca Journal, under Nathan Randall, A. E. Baraby, and J. H. Selkreg, ca. 1837; Onondaga County, stories of; Naming of Lake George; Guide to New York City during the Dewey Celebration; Southern California, notes of a traveler; Sam Patch's Jump Over the Genesee Falls, Rochester (Nov. 13, 1899)

did.unittitle: Newspaper clippings; various; many from Ithaca newspapers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 20 (formerly Harris 5135 G120); Early Masonry in Tompkins County, by Murray Poole: first meetings held (1808-181?) at the Luther Gee Inn, Aurora & Seneca Streets, Ithaca, and at the John Cantine ("Mansion") House, Brookton; Ithaca Merchants (1859); History of the Presbyterian Church; Tompkins County Infant Heir Fund; List of New buildings and houses, Ithaca (1899); Dewitt Historical Society officers and committees (Apr. 5, 1900)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 21 (formerly Harris 5135 D41); Clippings mostly undated and without byline; some historical incidents

did.unittitle: Newspaper clippings, almost entirely from central NY papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 22 (formerly Harris 5135 D42); Simeon DeWitt, stories about (Ithaca Democrat, May 9, 1901); "Where Dewitt died," Ithaca Daily News, with picture of the Clinton House); Death notices and obits.: Miss Cornelia Ackley (Jan. 18, 1896); Jesse Whitlock (June 6, 1901); Bush vs. D. L. and W. [Delaware, Lackawanna and Western] R.R.: Mrs. Bush awarded damages due to her husband's death from bridge collapse; Onondaga Board of Supervisors, First Meeting, May 27, 1794), undated story about; Pearson Estate litigation; list of early Ithacans; Marriage of Miss Bertha Genung and Carl Fundis; Mulks family members attend; Tompkins House sold (July 7, 1901, with picture); Cortland County's Richest and Most Eccentric Son (Syracuse Sunday Herald, Feb. 10, 1901); more on Rulloff case (Apr. 17, 1907); Dewitt Historical Society first annual meeting; Dewitt Historical Society first anniversary meeting, including a commendation of Charles F. Mulks (Ithaca Daily News, 19??)

did.unittitle: Early history of Caroline; newspaper clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 23 (formerly Harris 5135 D43; Mulks's signature on flyleaf); on both clipping-obscured and clipping-free pages, Mulks's own account of the history of and pioneers in Caroline Township, some possibly based on recollections by the Middaugh sisters; p. 28: Ithaca Fur Company skunk farm (New York Sun Sept. 9, 1895); p. 89: Businessmen in Slaterville; Supervisors meeting (1894); Town meetings; p. 111: Dewitt Clinton's Canal Journal of 1810; p. 125: Rev. Tyler's stories; Gen. Henry Bloom (Ithaca Journal, Jan. 29, 1896); J. A. McAlley's (McCauley) Elevator Fire (Oct . 1, 1903); Giles Street Bridge story; Prof. Estevan A. Fuertes receives $100,000 for designing the Sanitary System for Santos, Brazil

did.unittitle: Early history of Caroline; newspaper clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 24 (formerly Harris 5135 D44); Masonic symbolism, article on; Pres. Andrew Johnson's Impeachment trial, story on; Frances O. Thurston-Simeon S. Newell scandal; her suicide (Broome Gazette, Whitney's Point, Feb. 26, 1868)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 25 (formerly Harris 5135 D45); [original guide provides no additional contents]

did.unittitle: Caroline records; Politics; Black voters; newspaper clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 26 (formerly Harris 5135 D46); Early Caroline records, 1820-1830; Politics in Caroline; Intimidation of Black voters (1878); Gen. Cantine's Waverly tract; Time table of early Owego to Cayuga transportation (railroad; steamboat, carriage); "A Reliable Cipher"; Slaterville School entertainment, including students Jay Phillips, Harry Davis, Omar Mulks, Charles Northrup, Mary Crispell, Susie Slater, and Sallie Duell (March 29, 1879)

did.unittitle: Newspaper clippings; manuscript notes by Mulks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 27 (formerly Harris 5135 D47); Clippings almost all of column entitled "Replies and Decisions," from an unidentified commerce and finance periodical, probably out of New York City (1900-1904); Manuscript notes by Charles Mulks, 1894 and later, including: about the Amana Society, Iowa, from a May, 1900 article in Chautauqua Magazine; First description of the Town of Caroline, from the 1813 edition of Spafford's Gazetteer of the State of New York; Mulks's interview of Sextus B. Landon of Brookton, at Noxon's Store, Tues., Nov. 1, 1904, about the 1841 settling in Caroline of Landon's father, from Pine Hill, Ulster County

did.unittitle: Newspaper clippings, primarly a series entitled "The Susquehanna Valley"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 28 (formerly Harris 5153 D48); "The Susquehanna Valley" originally published in the monthly "St. Nicholas,'" at the Owego Gazette, 1853-54; reissued in 1900 by Owego Gazette editor, Charles P. Avery; Genealogy of the Ellis Family of Ellis; Ann H. Smith Will contest (1900); "Result of a Doctor's Joke," re contest of wits between Dr. Eaddie and the Ithaca Board of Health during a diphtheria epidemic (Ithaca dispatch in the New York Sun, March 29, 1900); Marriage of Eva-Dawn Willsey to L. B. Lormore at Ellis (June 13, 1900)

did.unittitle: Newspaper clippings, more than half consisting of poetry

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 29 (formerly Harris 5135 D49); on flyleaf: "Centre Lisle, Broome County, New York, May 1, 1876"); "Tioga County from 1784-1876" (series from Waverly Advocate); "Historical Record of Ithaca and Tompkins County" (from unidentified newspaper, read by Horace Mack, 1876); [per old typewritten guide: "Mostly deals with Cornell 1911-1912: stories about Proctor {Theodore H.) Tweston, student-town clashes, undergrad hi-jinks, etc."]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: History of Caroline, Slaterville, Dryden, and Ithaca; populations of free and enslave African Americans in the region; notes on Indigenous peoples; genealogies of many local families; deeds and other legal papers; early transportation; interviews.

did.unittitle: Notes on pioneers of New York State from Annals of Albany; Pre-Revolutionary and Revolutionary records; notes on source materials; etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook 1 (formerly Mss Ii 77; also marked Vol. 302); Principal topics Caroline Township, Ithaca, and Tompkins County; p;. 12-13: Stage lines; First Episcopal Church in Ithaca; p. 18: Population of Caroline in 1830: 2033; Postmasters in 1850: Julius M. Ackley (Ithaca); Samuel P. Ashley (Speedsville); S. E. Green (Slaterville); p. 23: Tjerck Claessen DeWitt, ancestor of Simeon DeWitt, and DeWitt genealogy; pp. 38-42: DeWitt and Dubois families; p. 43: 1825 Census: County of Tompkins, and parts of Tioga and Ulster Counties; pp. 43-50: Town, county and other public officers; pp. 51, 52: Military matters (1830); p. 54: 1830, the two newspapers in Tompkins County; p. 55: Tompkins County delegates to State Consitutional Convention (1821); pp. 55-58: Public officers, miscellaneous information about; p. 67: Amasa Dana, Tompkins Country Congressman, 28th Congress; 1845 Census: Caroline and Tompkins County; pp. 73-75: Rulloff's trial (1871); p. 77: Burial places of Simeon DeWitt; Erie Canal; Members of various courts; Richard Varick DeWitt, Sr.; Hermanus Bleeker; Abraham Bloodgood; Francis Bloodgood; Edward Edwards; Albany, Albany County; Binghamton, Broome County; Elmira, Chemung County; Utica, Oneida County

did.unittitle: Tompkins County; Census of slaves in Marbletown, Ulster County, N.Y., 1755; Indians; etc,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook 2 (formerly Mss Ii 78); also: Ithaca and Owego Rail Road (sources for accounts); Notes on politics and lawyers; New York and Erie Telegraph, 1849-1850; Genealogy of the Cornell family; Anna Mulks; Ezra Cornell; Asheville, North Carolina

did.unittitle: History and records of the Town of Caroline; etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook 3 (formerly Mss Ii 79); Town of Caroline: List of residents (1802); Assessments (1813); Pioneers and inhabitants, also of Slaterville; Military enrollment (1862); (on p. 31): List of farmers owning over 200 acres, and their taxes (1864); Records of Robison, Whitewater, Speedsville, and Brookton cemeteries; Supervisors of Tompkins County (1825); Tompkins County Patrons Relief Association; Extent of the Watkins and Flint Purchase of 1794; Bull Tavern; Tavern licenses; Wisconsin pioneers and holdings of family in Wisconsin; Genealogy of Mulks family; Estate of Daniel J. Shaw; Land transactions; Source books; Rulloff; pp. 1-4: Speed, Cantine, and other early Caroline families; pp. 7-33: Other Caroline families; p. 38: other Caroline families, and ones of Ithaca and Danby; Leet family; Lewis Beers; Daniel S. Shaw; Military roll; Samuel Johnson; Humphrey family

did.unittitle: History of early inhabitants of Caroline and Ithaca; enealogies; property deeds; etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook 4 (formerly Mss Ii 80; also marked Vol. 305; dated Dec. 3, 1895); Tax roll of Caroline (1887); Account of Michigan Land Company (1824); Will of Ezra Cornell; Notes on northern part of Ithaca and Dryden (1813-1817), including the following families: Ayres, Bates, Beers, Bennett, Collins, Cornell,, Delamater, Peleg Ellis, Gee, Gunderman, Halsey, Abraham Johnson, McCormick, Garret Mandeville, Meddaugh, Benoni Mulks, John Mullex (Mullocks), Wm. T. Southworth, Speed, Thompson, and Tillotson; p. 14: DeWitt Family of Ulster County, by Thomas G. Evans (1890); Vol. 9 from Aunt Ann Ellis's Birthday Book, including death dates of John M. Smith and Eva D. Wilsey; Deeds (primarily in Ithaca, unless noted otherwise): p. 15: Ackley to Morey (ca. 1850); p. 17: Charles Mulks to Jane Northrup (old Boice Farm, Jemima Schoonmaker lot (Jan. 25, 1853); p. 18 Charles Mulks to Peter Mulks (Dryden Wood lot, Jan. 25, 1853); p. 19: Henry Ackley to Ira Bower (Nov. 11, 1833); p. 23: Simeon DeWitt to Henry Ackley (Aug. 18, 1825; recorded by Miles Finch, Dept. Clerk); p. 25: Cayuga Manufacturing Company to Henry Ackley and Henry Hibbard (Aug. 17, 1828); p. 26: Henry Bower to Henry Ackley (1826)

did.unittitle: Slavery in New York State, 1790-1850; treaties with Indians; early inhabitants of Tompkins and Tioga Counties; etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook 5 (formerly Mss Ii 81); Census of Free Blacks and Slaves in Tompkins County (1820); In 1820, 32 slaves lived in Caroline, then part of Tioga County; Origins of Dryden and Speedsville; Legends of early inhabitants of Slaterville, Speedsville, and Ulster County; Folklore of Mulks family; Complete Assessment Roll of Caroline Township for the year 1813; Settlement of Ellis Hollow, 1804-1816, by Ellis, Hurd, Parmenter, Gosper, and Smith families; Settlement of Rawson Hollow, 1802, by Lyman Rawson and Samuel Blackman; Stories about Augustus Boyer, Mr. and Mrs. Hartman Ennist from Ulster County, Robert Freeland, Bartholomew Green, Henry D. Green, John Edward Green, Thomas Heggie, "General" Robert Hyde, William Jackson,Thomas McLeese, the Patillos, George Pumpelly, Joseph Speed, and William Thomas; Notes on the following families: Clinton, Dain, Gunderman, Halliday, Levi Hawkins, Heath, Samuel Dean Hutchings, Garret and Reuben Mandeville, James Personius, William Rounseville, Whittaker, and Williams; Dutch Reformed Church of Caroline, early members of; Stories of cottage prayer meetings in Mott's Corners and Brearley Hill; Settlement of the James R. Speed estate (1857)

did.unittitle: Slaves and slave owners in Caroline; notes on Indians; notes on New York State law; etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook 6 (formerly Mss Ii 82); First Land Office, at Canandaigua (1789); New York representation at Constitutional Convention in Philadelphia (1787); Election contests; Town meetings; School meetings; Cantine Location Patent; Dutch Settlement; County Divisions, Clinton family genealogy; Various legal cases; p. 25: Slavery in Caroline; p. 18: Members of Assembly, 1844; Peter Lounsbery of Mott's Corners on Colleges, Academies, and Schools Committee; p. 31: John Cantine voted against ratification of the Constitution of the United States by the New York State Convention (1788); Transfers of various deeds, Ithaca and Caroline; pp. 79-92; 103-108: Elections; Fred E. Bates vs. Robert G. Speed, et al.); References to: Andries Bevier; Charles Clinton; Sir George Clinton; Oliver Phelps

did.unittitle: Interviews with, recollections of, and genealogies of early settlers in vicinity of Ithaca; etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook 7 (formerly Mss Ii 83); pp. 1-18: Interviews with: John Marsh, former Gov. Alonzo B. Cornell. William Linn DeWitt, John H. Selkreg, Lucius G. Mix; Family histories: Moses Cantine (Cantyne), Bartholomew Green, Benjamin Pelton of Fredericksburg, Dutchess County, N.Y., Himspaugh, Cook, Jennings of Danby, John Pumpelly, Lewis Beers, Beardsley, Rev. Samuel Parker, Tyler, Rev. Theodore L. Cuyler, Benjamin Ledyard Cuyler, James Eddy, Otis Eddy, William A. Gunderman; Other subjects: Huguenots, Telegraph, Grape culture, Revolutionary War; Militia in 1835, War of 1812, Military Tract-Ulysses; Aurora, Cayuga County; New Paltz, Ulster County; Danby, Tompkins County; Marbletown, Ulster County; Folklore

did.unittitle: Town of Caroline geography, history; Slavery and slaveholders; interviews with early residents; etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook 8 (formerly Mss Ii 84); Johnson Lands and Cantine Tract; Land transactions in Ithaca; Indian settlements; Hugnenot settlers; Notes from early issues of Ithaca Journal and Advertiser (1829-1831); New York State Militia; pp. 3-18: Pioneers from Virginia who brought their slaves, 1803 on; p. 21: Early stage coach mail lines (1820); Route no. 43, Owego to Ithaca, via Berkshire; Land transactions involving State Street business properties; p. 37: Tompkins County Infant Heir Fund annual report (Nov. 1886); p. 39: Personius family reunion, Ithaca (Aug. 23, 1899), including Isaiah (Joyous) Personius; p. 71: Fortner's on Owego and Ithaca Turnpike, renamed "Travellers' Abode" by Charles Bush; Mulks interviews with late 19th century Tompkins County residents: Capt. Tilton, Daniel Slaughter, A.[?] C.[?] Sanford, Francis King, Mrs. Faville, H. G. Goodrich, James Quigg, Peleg Ellis, Artemus Tyler, Charles Alexander Hart, Thomas J.[?] McIlheny, William Pitt Beers, John McGavan, Hawkins, William Ross Humphrey; p. 100: Humhrey family genealogy; Gunderman (New Jersey Gunterman) family; Notes from: James H. Hotchkins, History of the purchase and settlement of western New York (1848) and Marcus Schoonmaker, History of Kingston, N.Y. (1888); Other names and subjects, including: Mrs. Hurd, Henry W. Sage, Mattys Blanchan, Mott Robertson, Ira Tillotson, Hon. Charles Humphrey, Robert McDowell, Thomas Paine; Erie Canal, Battle of Lake Erie, 1812

did.unittitle: Early history of pioneers to Caroline and Dryden; etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook 9 (formerly Mss Ii 85; Mulks's note on flyleaf: "This book is meant to present facts, figures, and reliable narratives of the history of the town of Caroline and is the second book I have devoted to that use and purpose. Over 42 families are considered"); Cantine family; Early postmasters and supervisors; Town clerks of Caroline; Bush tavern; Genealogical notes and other recollections; Additional local names: Jacobus Depuy, Aaron Cass, Amarah Yates, Maria Johnson Earsley, John Pattio, David Rich, Aaron Bull, Ambrose Bull, Thomas M. Bozer [Boyer?], Hugh Bozer (Boyer), John Schutt, Francis H. Monroe, Sabin Mann, Silas Hutchinson, Peter Smith, Samuel Tobey, Samuel H. Dean, Matthew Jansen, Joseph Chambers, Michael Krum, Henry Krum, John Rounsvell, Sam Rounsvell, Cornelius Freer, Levi Freer, Levi Slater, Lyman Rawson, Hon. R. H. S. Hyde, James Ashley, Robert Freeland, De Mandeville family, Garret Mandeville

did.unittitle: Origins of Tompkins County; Pioneers to Caroline; Slavery in Caroline; etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook 10 (formerly Mss Ii 86; First 21 pages are day book records); Peter Webb, slave from Virginia, purchases his freedom from John J. Speed (1818); Origins of N.Y.S. counties, and their populations in 1731; First settlements of Ithaca by Jacob and John Yaple, Isaac and John Dumond, and Peter Hinnepaugh[Himepaugh/Hinepau]; Military Tract created by treaty with the Iroquois (1789); Transfer of New York territory by Massachusetts; Members of Assembly, Tompkins County (1817-1870); Ithaca and Owego Rail Road; First public road in Tompkins Country (1792); Cayuga and Seneca Canal; Alterations of county boundaries; County judges, district attorneys, sheriffs, clerks; Pastors of Methodist Episcopal Church of Slaterville (1849-1869); General accounts; Produce; Work

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook 11 (formerly Mss Ii 87; Flyleaf signed by Mulks, Dec. 24, 1871); Early settlers of Caroline; First school in Caroline; Aaron Cass; Jerry "Tomlinson;" Other names; Reminiscences of Lisle

did.unittitle: Early accounts of Ithaca, Ulysses, Dryden, Lisle, and Danby; etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook 12 (formerly Mss Ii 88); Assessment roll for Ithaca(1829); David Ayres and his South Hill land development, Ithaca (1818); Benjamin Pelton; John Smith; John L. Hance; Levi Beers; John Patterson; General Henry Bloom of Lansing, member of the N.Y.S. Council of Appointment (1825); Revolutionary War; Thomas Paine, Mills; Folklore

did.unittitle: Early history of Ithaca; Notes about New York colonial history; etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook 13 (formerly Mss Ii 89); Excerpts from Dr. Samuel J. Parker's Reminiscences of Ithaca (manuscript); Why General Simeon DeWitt selected Ithaca; Ithaca's early doctors and lawyers; Notes from Calendar of New York colonial manuscripts, indorsed land papers; in the office of the Secretary of State of New York, 1643-1803 (Albany: Weed, Parsons & Co., 1864)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents regarding land purchases; promissory notes; genealogies; biography of Charles F. Mulks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gaylord Commie {Connor/ Connue?], to Benjamin Thomas, Oblong [town in northeast Dutchess County?], 2 {pounds?] 18 s. 5 d.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: John Mulks to Lowman and Castle. Witnessed by Peter Stryker and Silas Baldwin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: John Mulks of Esopus, Ulster County, to Messrs. Jacob Lowman and Frederick Castle, both of Chemung, then Tioga County, for 325 acres of land in Tioga County, comprising the easterly part of a tract containing 500 acres granted to John Cantine. Recorded Tioga County; Is mortgage for security of payment of $1075 for said land.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lowman and Castle to John Mulks, made Sept. 30, 1800 and recorded June 18, 1801.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Joseph Luddington [Ludenton], to William Gage and Stephen Titus, Town of Dover, Dutchess County.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William R. Phelps and Philo Gregory, to William Thomas. N.P.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: John Mulks, Town of Caroline, then Tioga County, and Charles Mulks. In consideration of the sum of ten cents, John releases to Charles his right to property in the Town of Dryden. Witnessed by Levi Slater and Isaac Stilwell.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Whereby Charles Mulks releases to John Mulks one-half the sawmill lot in Town of Caroline. Witnessed by Slater and Stilwell.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Charles Mulks of Caroline to James Roosevelt of New York City, involving land in Caroline (Lot no. 15 in NE section of Township No. 10 in the Twelve Townships of Watkins and Flint Purchase); Recorded in Tompkins County Clerk's Office.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Levi Slater to Charles Mulks, for land in Caroline.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Thomas Robertson, Tompkins County Sheriff, to James Mulks, for property in the Town of Ulysses. Formerly property of James Matthews, sold to satisfy a judgment had against him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: James Hall of New York City to Charles Mulks, for land in Cantine's location in Caroline, formerly owned by Levi Slater.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Whereby Samuel Love, William Tefft, and Benjamin Ferris agree to sell land in Caroline to Charles Mulks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Whereby Jacob Robison, formerly of Caroline, but now of Tioga County, Pennsylvania, released land in Caroline to Charles Mulks. Recorded in Steuben County.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To land in Caroline, S. Love, B. Ferris, W. Tefft to Charles Mulks. Recorded in Tompkins County.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To land in Caroline, S. Love, B. Ferris, W. Tefft to Charles Mulks. Recorded in Tompkins County.

did.unittitle: Erie Canal Directory and Rate Schedule

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pasted to inside of a heavy cloth folder apparently meant to hold weigh-lock slips.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Granted to Hiram Hollister as "rectifier of spirits," Town of Dryden, Tompkins County, N.Y. Issued at Binghamton; signed by S. C. Hitchcock, collector of the 26th Collection District.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Granted to Peter Mulks, Dryden, as "Manufacturer."

did.unittitle: Estate of Milton Phillips, of Sheshequin Township, Bradford County, Pennsylvania: documents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From Milton Phillips's brother and heir, Archibald D. Phillips, Benton County, Iowa, to George W. Phillips of Sheshequin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From Milton Phillips's brother and heir, Henry Phillips, Lisbon, Waukesha County, Wisconsin, to George W. Phillips of Sheshequin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From Milton Phillips's brother and heir, Harrison Phillips, Rio, Columbia County, Wisconsin, to George W. Phillips of Sheshequin.

did.unittitle: Invoices of goods from the Milton Phillips estate, sold at vendue

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook kept by George W. Phillips; gives prices of ladders, sleighs, other farm equipment, household goods, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 pcs.; probably the family of Benjamin Thomas of village of Oblong, Dutchess County; names include: Abraham, Benjamin (1748-1816), Caty[?}, Elisabeth, two Elizas, Isaac, two Johns, Maria, Martha, Mehitabel, Patience, Sarah, Tabor, two Williams.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Of the 75-acre holding of Isaac[?] Stilwell, presumably in the town of Caroline.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Belonging to Mary Elizabeth Dusenberry, age 75, Slaterville.

did.unittitle: Items relating to Charles F. Mulks
Biographical sketch of Mulks by Jane P. M. Vaughan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift of Rhoda M. Durkan, 1985
